neighborhoods.name: Historic District
neighborhoods.description: America's oldest city with Spanish colonial architecture, Flagler College, and St. George Street pedestrian zone
neighborhoods.character: Oldest city, Spanish colonial, pedestrian zone

housing.predominantType: Historic houses, condos, and renovated colonial buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of St. Augustine. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living St. Augustine

neighborhoods.name: Ponte Vedra Beach
neighborhoods.description: Upscale beach community with TPC Sawgrass, luxury resorts, and A-rated schools
neighborhoods.character: Upscale beach, TPC golf, luxury resorts

housing.predominantType: Luxury homes, beach condos, and golf community properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to St. Augustine's center and services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living St. Augustine

neighborhoods.name: Hastings / Elkton
neighborhoods.description: Inland farming communities with very affordable housing and agricultural character

housing.predominantType: Modest houses, mobile homes, and farm properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living St. Augustine

neighborhoods.name: World Golf Village / Palencia
neighborhoods.description: Master-planned communities with golf, A-rated schools, and family-oriented amenities

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes in planned golf communities

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living St. Augustine
